_meta.source_lines: apply_spec table (per-family entries derived from each tool's GetInputSchema and applicator)
_meta.keying_convention: top-level keys equal each tool's GetCategory(); look up via tool_search(name=<family>_apply_spec, detail="full")

attenuation.calling_convention: flat: kind="Attenuation", asset_path, properties at top-level; one-shot stateless apply
attenuation.id_mapping_pattern: n/a -- single asset target identified by asset_path
gotchas: Non-session: no session_id required and no editor session is opened
Properties go through FProperty::ImportText_Direct; struct properties accept either nested objects or canonical text form
Asset is auto-created at asset_path if absent

behaviortree.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: BT property values go through FProperty::ImportText_Direct; FValueOrBBKey_* properties auto-wrap a bare scalar as (DefaultValue=X), so 'WaitTime': '0.5' works
Class names are fuzzy-resolved (drop U/F prefix; partial matches allowed)
Spec is additive: applying to a BT that already has a root selector results in two trees under the BT root
Non-session behaviortree_inspect only walks the primary root child; use session-mode (open->structure->close) to see additions

blackboard.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: Each key requires id, name, type
type must be a valid blackboard key type string (Object, Bool, Float, Int, String, Vector, Rotator, Name, Class, Enum)

bp.calling_convention: flat: asset_path, spec passed at top-level (matches the existing schema)
bp.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: This is the ONLY apply_spec tool that auto-creates the asset; the other tools require the asset to already exist (duplicate first)
Connection pin names are fuzzy-resolved: 'exec' (input) and 'then' (output) work without knowing the canonical pin name
Class names are fuzzy-resolved (drop U/F prefix; partial matches allowed)
spec.graph names the target graph (e.g. 'EventGraph')

concurrency.calling_convention: flat: kind="Concurrency", asset_path, properties at top-level; one-shot stateless apply
concurrency.id_mapping_pattern: n/a -- single asset target identified by asset_path
gotchas: Non-session: no session_id required and no editor session is opened
Properties go through FProperty::ImportText_Direct

eqs.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: Each option requires id, generator, and tests[]
Generator and test type strings are fuzzy-resolved (e.g. 'EnvQueryGenerator_SimpleGrid', 'EnvQueryTest_Distance')

level_sequence.calling_convention: params={asset_path, spec, optional session_id}; opens its own session when session_id is not provided
level_sequence.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: Asset must already exist; apply is non-destructive in that bindings/tracks/sections/keyframes are added/modified atomically
spec.playback_range is optional; omit to keep the existing range

material.calling_convention: params={asset_path, spec, optional session_id}; opens its own session when session_id is not provided
material.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: Asset must already exist; duplicate a template first if you need a new material
Connection endpoints reference expressions by spec id; pin names are matched as authored

material_instance.calling_convention: params={asset_path, spec, optional session_id}; opens its own session when session_id is not provided
material_instance.id_mapping_pattern: n/a -- single asset target identified by asset_path
gotchas: Operation is instance_apply_spec; composed wire name is material_instance_instance_apply_spec (doubled instance is intentional under current category+operation naming)
Asset must already exist; instance pointing at the named parent is created by spec.parent
Clearing a parameter and overriding it in the same call: clears run first

metasound.calling_convention: flat: kind="MetaSoundSource", asset_path, spec body at top-level; one-shot stateless apply
metasound.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: Non-session: no session_id required and no editor session is opened
Creates or locates the asset, then routes the spec body to the MetaSound applicator

niagara.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: Niagara emitters[] is NOT yet implemented; only parameters[] mutates the asset (emitter creation requires Niagara template-system integration)
The applicator returns no error for emitters[] entries today; treat them as a stub
Each parameter requires id, name, type, value (where type is float, FLinearColor, FVector, etc.)

pcg.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: PCG connection pin names are EXACT-match (no fuzzy resolution yet for PCG-specific port aliases)
Each connection requires source_node, source_pin, target_node, target_pin

soundclass.calling_convention: flat: kind="SoundClass", asset_path, properties, child_classes at top-level; one-shot stateless apply
soundclass.id_mapping_pattern: n/a -- single asset target identified by asset_path; child_classes by SoundClass path
gotchas: Non-session: no session_id required and no editor session is opened
child_classes references SoundClass asset paths that must exist

soundcue.calling_convention: flat: kind="SoundCue", asset_path, nodes/connections at top-level; one-shot stateless apply
soundcue.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: Non-session: no session_id required and no editor session is opened
Two-pass cross-reference handling is preserved: referenced assets load first, then the cue graph is wired

soundmix.calling_convention: flat: kind="SoundMix", asset_path, envelope, class_adjusters at top-level; one-shot stateless apply
soundmix.id_mapping_pattern: n/a -- single asset target identified by asset_path; class_adjusters by SoundClass path
gotchas: Non-session: no session_id required and no editor session is opened
class_adjusters references SoundClass asset paths that must exist

statetree.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: Transitions can be authored either per-state (states[i].transitions) or top-level (spec.transitions[]) -- both forms are equivalent and resolve from/to via id_mappings
Top-level transitions are useful when authoring transitions independently of state definitions
Each state requires id, name, type, parent (null for root)

widgetbp.id_mapping_pattern: spec ids are looked up in id_mappings, not by name
gotchas: WidgetBP applicator opens existing Widget Blueprints only (no auto-create); duplicate a template and apply against the duplicate
Each widget requires id, type, parent (null for root), and optional children[] / properties / slot_properties
